pairs.question: What was the name of the English rugby union competition for the 2007–08 season in the fourth division (south)?
pairs.answer: The competition was called the National Division Three South.

pairs.question: Which team won the 2007–08 National Division Three South season and secured promotion?
pairs.answer: Mounts Bay won the league and secured promotion to the 2008–09 National Division Two.

pairs.question: What was the promotion playoff format for the runners-up in the 2007–08 National Division Three South season?
pairs.answer: The runners-up played a one-game playoff against the runners-up from National Division Three North. The match was held at the home ground of the club with the superior league record.

pairs.question: How many successive promotions did Mounts Bay achieve before the 2007–08 season?
pairs.answer: Mounts Bay was part of a sequence of six successive promotions, with the streak beginning with Cornwall 1 in the 2003–04 season.

pairs.question: Which three teams were relegated from the 2007–08 National Division Three South season?
pairs.answer: Luton, North Walsham, and Clifton were relegated. Luton and North Walsham went to London Division 1, while Clifton dropped to South West Division 1.

pairs.question: Write a Python function that determines the final standings of the 2007–08 National Division Three South season based on the provided text.
